column7: Attention-based architecture for heterogeneous graphs. Node- and edge-type-dependent attention. Foundation for HAN/HGMAE.
Self-supervised pretraining on heterogeneous graphs via masked autoencoding. Our primary method.

column2: Finding Money Launderers Using Heterogeneous GNNs
column7: First GNN on large-scale real-world heterogeneous bank data (DNB, Norway). Novel edge-aggregation.

column2: Heterogeneous Graph Auto-Encoder for Credit Card Fraud
column7: Heterogeneous graph + autoencoder. AUC-PR 0.89, F1 0.81. Same tripartite topology as ours.

column2: Anti-Money Laundering by Group-Aware Deep Graph Learning
column7: Community-centric encoder for gang-level laundering detection. Tested on real bank card alliance data.
Heterogeneous GNN detector + explainer for e-commerce fraud. Scales to 1.1B nodes / 3.7B edges. VLDB 2022.

column2: AML Alert Optimization Using ML with Graphs
column7: Entity-centric + graph features for AML alert triage. 80% FP reduction, >90% TP on real data.

column2: Powerful GNNs for Money Laundering Detection
column7: GNNs on ~1M node / ~9M edge AMLSim data. Ego IDs, heterogeneous message passing, port numbering.

column2: Detecting and Preventing Money Laundering Using Deep Learning
column7: Hybrid LSTM-GraphSAGE. 95.4% accuracy on simulated data.

column2: LaundroGraph: Self-Supervised Graph Learning for AML
column7: First fully self-supervised AML system. Bipartite graph + link prediction. +12pp AUC.

column2: Privacy-Aware Graph Embeddings for AML Pipelines
column7: GNNs + homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving AML.

column2: Advances in Continual Graph Learning for AML
column7: Continual learning for GNN-based AML. Replay, regularization, architecture strategies.

column2: GNNs for Financial Fraud Detection: A Review
column7: Comprehensive review of 100+ GNN studies for financial fraud.

column2: Fraud Detection Using Deep Learning and SHAP Analysis
column7: Tabular feed-forward NN + SHAP. Not graph-based.

column2: AI-Powered Fraud Detection: GNN Compliance and Risk
Enhancing AML Systems Using Knowledge Graphs and GNNs
column7: KG + GCN/GAT for AML. GAT overfitting issues. Timestamps critical.

column2: ML in Transaction Monitoring: The Prospect of xAI
column7: xAI requirements depend on liable party in TM. CBS paper.
